prompt: Create a highly detailed photorealistic close-up of a handcrafted character candy sculpture: one cute anime-style schoolgirl made entirely from glossy pulled sugar and hard candy, displayed on one thin brass support rod above a round wooden base in a warm artisan candy workshop. The face is intentionally covered by one plain soft peach square mask with no features. The character has translucent pastel blue, pink, and white hair formed from many thin shiny strands of sugar, with looped side locks and tiny flower-like candy ornaments. She wears a translucent cream jacket over a frilly blue-and-pink skirt, all rendered as clear amber, pink, and blue candy with sparkling edges, bubbles, seams, and glassy highlights. She holds exactly one small golden teddy bear candy in both arms; the bear has two round ears, two dark bead eyes, a pale muzzle, and rounded paw pads. On her back is exactly one glossy transparent pink school backpack with a small blue bow and a tiny bear charm. Include exactly one visible pair of black-handled scissors on the wooden table in the lower left, exactly two slim silver candy-working rods in the lower right foreground, exactly one black tool cup at the right rear, exactly six round candy pieces or lollipops in the blurred background, and exactly three pastel striped candy pieces on the front-right tray. Use a shallow depth of field, warm golden lighting, bokeh highlights, macro product photography realism, intricate refractive caustics, wet-looking sugar shine, and a cozy wooden studio atmosphere. Avoid resin, glass, plastic, or porcelain; the object should clearly feel like master-level traditional candy art.
